data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $160 million yesterday, while none of the twelve ETFs saw a net inflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $160 million yesterday (September 5th, US Eastern Time). The Blackrock IBIT ETF saw the largest daily net outflow, with a net outflow of $63.206 million. IBIT's total net inflow has now reached $58.741 billion. The next largest daily net outflow was the Bitwise BITB ETF, with a net outflow of $49.6455 million. BITB's total net inflow has now reached $2.211 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $144.045 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to the total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.48%, and cumulative net inflows reaching $54.487 billion.

data.title: Significant Outflow from U.S. Ethereum Spot ETFs Reported
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Farside Investors indicates a substantial net outflow of $446 million from U.S. Ethereum spot ETFs. The breakdown of the outflows includes BlackRock's ETHA with a decrease of $309.9 million, Fidelity's FETH experiencing a reduction of $37.8 million, 21Shares' TETH seeing a drop of $14.7 million, Grayscale's ETHE losing $51.8 million, and Grayscale's Mini ETH recording a decline of $32.6 million.

data.title: Analyst: Bitcoin no longer relies on the halving cycle, and those who predict that the price will reach a peak in Q4 may have misunderstood the probability
data.description: According to Cointelegraph, crypto analyst PlanC wrote on the X platform that anyone who believes Bitcoin will peak in the fourth quarter of this year doesn't understand statistics or probability. From a statistical and probabilistic perspective, this is like flipping a coin and getting tails three times in a row, then betting everything on the fourth halving. However, relying on the first three halving cycles doesn't provide statistically significant data. Today, with the rise of Bitcoin treasuries and the influx of funds into US spot Bitcoin ETFs, the halving cycle is no longer relevant to Bitcoin. Beyond psychological, self-fulfilling prophecies, there's no fundamental reason to explain Bitcoin's peak in the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: The US SEC has postponed its decision on the Grayscale DOT spot ETF to November 8th.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) will extend its review of Nasdaq’s application to list the Grayscale Polkadot Trust (DOT). The commission will extend the deadline for approving or disapproving the proposed rule change by another 60 days, with a final decision date set for November 8.

data.title: A whale transferred 3,500 ETH to OKX in three days, resulting in a potential loss of $155,000.
data.description: According to monitoring by @ai_9684 (xtpa), the "ETH ICO 1 Million ETH Whale" established a 2,000 ETH position on September 2nd and has deposited a total of 3,500 ETH, worth $15.11 million, into OKX over the past three days. The price of ETH withdrawn from the exchange at that time was $4,396.23. If this portion of ETH was indeed a purchase, the loss would be $155,000.

data.title: Nasdaq's New Regulations on Digital Asset Treasury Unlikely to Affect Strategy
data.description: According to PANews, Strategy announced on the X platform that Nasdaq's newly established regulations concerning the Digital Asset Treasury (DAT) will not impact the company's strategic plans. The operations related to cryptocurrency ATMs and other capital market activities will continue as usual.

data.title: Arkham Identifies Unaccounted $5 Billion Bitcoin Linked to Movie2K
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Arkham has revealed on social media that it has identified $5 billion worth of Bitcoin that the German government failed to seize. Earlier in 2024, German authorities confiscated 49,858 Bitcoins from the operators of the Movie2K piracy website and sold them in July 2024 at an average price of $57,900, generating $2.89 billion.

Arkham's investigation uncovered another cluster of Bitcoins linked to Movie2K's early activities, totaling approximately 45,000 Bitcoins, currently valued at $4.99 billion. These Bitcoins have remained unmoved since 2019 and are distributed across more than 100 Bitcoin wallets, likely still under the control of Movie2K operators. The amount is nearly equivalent to the Bitcoins that were seized.

The German government has not mentioned this additional batch of Bitcoins nor included them in the initial "emergency sale" plan.

data.title: The decentralized asset token layer built on Hyperliquid has exceeded $1 billion in Unit TVL.
data.description: Messari data shows that since February, the total locked value (TVL) of Unit, a decentralized asset token layer built on Hyperliquid, has increased by 1529%, exceeding US$1 billion in August.

data.title: A whale shorted BTC after closing its ETH long position at a loss of $35.4 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale holding a 15x ETH long position has closed its position, losing $35.39 million, and opened a BTC short position with 25x leverage, buying 1,106.93 BTC worth $122.6 million.
